description_long: This is a compact submachine gun with a striking ancient Egyptian-inspired design, featuring hieroglyphic motifs and pharaoh imagery in gold and black across its body. The receiver and barrel shroud are adorned with stylized patterns, while the grip and magazine well display detailed illustrations, including a pharaoh's mask and other symbolic figures. The weapon has a short, cylindrical barrel with a vented muzzle device, and a simple iron sight system is visible on top. The stock is a minimalist, wireframe design, folding to the side for compactness. The overall finish is smooth and glossy, with the gold and black color scheme giving it a unique, decorative appearance. This rifle is likely constructed from metal and polymer, typical for modern submachine guns, with a lightweight and ergonomic build suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, gold-and-black submachine gun decorated with Egyptian motifs, featuring a folding wire stock and vented muzzle device. The weapon has a smooth, glossy finish and detailed hieroglyphic artwork.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muzzle, vented muzzle device, and pharaoh mask artwork on the magazine well.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the Egyptian motifs, detailed illustrations, and the folding wire stock.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the receiver, rear sight, and the wire stock folded to the side.
description_view_3: Right side view showing more hieroglyphic patterns, the trigger, and the magazine well artwork.
